USA.title: When starting out in real estate investing don't be the dumb ...
USA.transcript: I build my buy box and I focus on a specific niche. I look at any and every deal that comes to my table. I identify three specific zip codes that I want to invest in. I cast a wide net and look at deals in any neighborhood that looks sexy. I run my own comparables using Zillow, Redfin, and MLS. I put my full unwavering trust in the zestimate. I'm not emotional. If the numbers don't work, I'm onto the next property. I try to make the deal work because I really, really love the house.
USA.script:
Here is the script:

Most new investors lose money doing exactly this. They see a cute house and suddenly the numbers "make sense" somehow. I've watched it happen so many times it hurts.

Smart investors build a buy box first. Three zip codes max. Non negotiable criteria. They run their own comps on MLS not just Zillow. And when the numbers say no they literally walk away without blinking.

Emotional investors though? They're out here trying to force deals because the kitchen has original hardwood floors. That's not investing. That's an expensive crush.

The difference between investors who build real wealth and ones who break even after two years is almost always discipline not intelligence. You don't need to be a genius. You need a system.

I work with investors at both stages all the time.
